question: Which is the most optimal scheduling algorithm?
The real difficulty with SJF in short term scheduling is ____________

options.a: it is too good an algorithm
options.b: knowing the length of the next CPU request
options.c: it is too complex to understand

question: The FCFS algorithm is particularly troublesome for ____________
explanation: In a time sharing system, each user needs to get a share of the CPU at regular intervals.

question: Preemptive Shortest Job First scheduling is sometimes called ____________

options.c: HRRN scheduling – Highest Response Ratio Next
options.d: SRTN scheduling – Shortest Remaining Time Next

question: An SJF algorithm is simply a priority algorithm where the priority is ____________

options.b: the inverse of the predicted next CPU burst

explanation: The larger the CPU burst, the lower the priority.

question: Choose one of the disadvantages of the priority scheduling algorithm?

options.a: it schedules in a very complex manner
options.b: its scheduling takes up a lot of time
options.c: it can lead to some low priority process waiting indefinitely for the CPU

options.b: keeping track of what pages are currently residing in memory
options.c: keeping track of how many times a given page is referenced
options.d: increasing the priority of jobs to ensure termination in a finite time

question: A solution to the problem of indefinite blockage of low – priority processes is ____________
Which of the following scheduling algorithms gives minimum average waiting time?

Consider the following set of processes, the length of the CPU burst time given in milliseconds.

Process    Burst time
P1         6
P2         8
P3         7
P4         3

---
Assuming the above process being scheduled with the SJF scheduling algorithm.

options.a: The waiting time for process P1 is 3ms
options.b: The waiting time for process P1 is 0ms
options.c: The waiting time for process P1 is 16ms
options.d: The waiting time for process P1 is 9ms

explanation: SJF (non-preemptive) order is P4 (3), P1 (6), P3 (7), P2 (8), so P1 waits 3ms.
